prompt: The reflection call throws IllegalAccessException. What is the bug?
choices: getDeclaredField returns null for private fields and get() throws NPE
Accessing a private field via reflection requires calling f.setAccessible(true) before f.get(); without it, IllegalAccessException is thrown
getDeclaredField only works on public fields; use getField instead
String.value is a char[] and cannot be retrieved via Field.get()
explanation: getDeclaredField finds private fields, but the JVM still enforces access control when you call get(). You must call f.setAccessible(true) before accessing the field value. Without it, reflection throws IllegalAccessException. Fix: add f.setAccessible(true); before f.get("hello").
